action: Immediately halt lifting operations and secure cranes
rule_text: Suspend all external crane operations when significant wave height exceeds safety limits.

action: Alert port authority and prepare emergency response teams
rule_text: Initiate storm watch procedures during rapid atmospheric pressure decline.

action: Deny new vessel arrivals until period decreases
rule_text: Restrict vessel maneuvering during long swell periods to ensure mooring security.

action: Engage heating systems for critical dockside utilities
rule_text: Activate freeze protection systems when water temperature approaches freezing point.

action: Mandate frequent breaks and provide heated shelters
rule_text: Implement extreme cold weather safety protocols for personnel working outdoors.

action: Generate exception report for terminal manager
rule_text: Flag vessels exceeding maximum terminal length limits for manual review

action: Route to Pier B or C based on availability
rule_text: Assign small vessels to designated feeder berths automatically

action: Require tug escort and pilot supervision
rule_text: Trigger safety protocol for ultra-large container vessels
Escalate handling priority for mega-class vessels
Restrict access for vessels below minimum economic size for deep-sea terminals
Crane operations must be paused when crane productivity drops below 15 moves per hour for more than 2 consecutive hours.
Crane breakdown exceeding 60 minutes requires immediate maintenance dispatch and supervisor notification.
Berth productivity falling below 20 moves per hour triggers operational review and resource reallocation.
Vessel arrival delay exceeding 6 hours requires berth schedule reassignment and notification to terminal operator.
Yard peak occupancy exceeding 85% triggers overflow protocol and diverts containers to secondary storage areas.
Gate truck turn time exceeding 90 minutes requires investigation of congestion causes and possible appointment rescheduling.
Tide level below 2 feet restricts deep-draft vessel entry until conditions improve.
Wind gust exceeding 25 m/s triggers immediate crane boom-down procedure.
Maximum wind speed limit permitted for a Point Richards entry is 30-35 knots based on steady winds.
Maximum wind speed limit permitted for a Geelong departure is 25-30 knots based on steady winds.
